title: Automatic Storage, Recursion, VLAs, and Escaping Addresses · Object-Lifetime Lens: Alloca Portability Risk
summary: A recursive document walker succeeds in tests but overflows a constrained worker and returns pointers into expired frames. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem alloca portability risk increment in bounded stack-use redesign. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat alloca-like facilities as nonstandard stack allocation with lifetime, overflow, loop, and portability hazards.
Use variable-length arrays only under explicit positive bounds and stack budgets, with portable alternatives.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Alloca Portability Risk · Theory Alloca Portability Risk
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8537. Produce bounded C evidence to replace unbounded recursive work with bounded explicit storage; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat alloca-like facilities as nonstandard stack allocation with lifetime, overflow, loop, and portability hazards.

steps.title: Build Alloca Portability Risk evidence · Theory Alloca Portability Risk
steps.instruction:
